title: GPT-5.5 is OpenAI's most capable agentic AI model yet
summary: GPT-5.5 is OpenAI's most capable agentic AI model yet - AI News GPT-5.5 is OpenAI’s most capable agentic AI model yet April 29, 2026 Share this story: Tags: agentic workflowschatgptopenai Categories: World of Work OpenAI launched GPT-5.5 on April 23 as what it calls “a new class of i
content: GPT-5.5 is OpenAI's most capable agentic AI model yet - AI News [...] GPT-5.5 is OpenAI’s most capable agentic AI model yet [...] OpenAI launched GPT-5.5 on April 23 as what it calls “a new class of intelligence for real work and powering agents,” and the framing is deliberate. OpenAI says it’s the most capable agentic AI model to date, built from the ground up to plan, use tools, check its own output, and work through tasks independently. [...] GPT-5.5 is the first retrained base model since GPT-4.5, co-designed with NVIDIA’s GB200 and GB300 NVL72 rack-scale systems. The company says the practical difference is that when using GPT5.5, tasks that previously required multiple prompts and human ‘course-correction’ can now be handed off more completely. The model is rolling out to Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise users in ChatGPT and Codex. API access followed on April 24. [...] GPT-5.5 Pro, available to Pro, Business, and Enterprise users, is priced at US$30 per million input tokens and US$180 per million output tokens. It applies additional parallel test-time compute on harder problems and leads the list of publicly-available models on BrowseComp, OpenAI’s agentic web-browsing benchmark, at 90.. [...] Greg Brockman described the release as “a real step forward towards the kind of computing that we expect in the future,” and chief scientist Jakub Pachocki noted the last two years of model progress had felt “surprisingly slow.” [...] GPT-5. [...] matches GPT-5. [...] ’s per-token latency in production serving while performing at a higher level of intelligence; larger, more capable models are often slower to serve, but that trade-off was avoided here. [...] Whether the benchmark leads translate into production gains for teams running real agentic pipelines is the question that will take the next few weeks to answer properly. The Terminal-Bench score is promising for unattended terminal agents and DevOps automation. The MCP Atlas gap is worth watching for anyone building heavily on tool-use orchestration. [...] GPT-5.5 is OpenAI’s most capable agentic AI model yet

title: Anthropic announces Claude Security public beta to find and fix software vulnerabilities
summary: Anthropic announces Claude Security public beta to find and fix software vulnerabilities - SiliconANGLE SHARE UPDATED 13:00 EDT / APRIL 30 2026 SECURITY Anthropic announces Claude Security public beta to find and fix software vulnerabilities SHARE Anthropic PBC announced the launch of Clau
content: Anthropic announces Claude Security public beta to find and fix software vulnerabilities - SiliconANGLE [...] Anthropic announces Claude Security public beta to find and fix software vulnerabilities [...] Anthropic PBC announced the launch of Claude Security in public beta mode today to help cybersecurity teams scan their codebases for vulnerabilities and generate patches. [...] Part of Claude Enterprise, the company’s subscription model aimed at large organizations, Security began as a research preview as Claude Code Security in February. Since its introduction, the company said hundreds of organizations have used it to discover and fix exploits in production code, including vulnerabilities existing tools had missed for years. [...] The new security tool is powered by the company’s flagship artificial intelligence model, Opus 4.7, as a dedicated defensive product, part of a longer-term trend for the company as a cybersecurity angle. [...] Anthropic recently launched Project [...] wing, debuting the use of the company’s [...] as a security [...] adept at discovering vulnerabilities [...] initiative, the company said customers can now work with partners building on [...] ’s security capabilities. This [...] such as CrowdStrike Holdings Inc., Palo Alto Networks Inc., SentinelOne Inc., Trend Micro Inc.’s TrendAI and Wiz Inc., integrating Opus 4.7 into [...] cybersecurity platforms. [...] Anthropic said that with these new capabilities, Claude can now reason over entire codebases by thinking similarly to the way a cybersecurity researcher does. Instead of looking for known patterns, it traces data flows, reads source code and examines interactions between code components and files to synthesize network effects. [...] The model then verifies everything with a confidence rating before it reaches an analyst so that it can provide more trustworthy outputs. Anthropic also said the model will do its homework as it goes, explaining its reasoning along the way, its confidence factors, the likelihood that a vulnerability can be exploited, the triage factors and how effective a fix will be. [...] From there, users can open a Claude Code session to apply the fix directly in context, with no more waiting days to go back and forth with security and engineering teams to get it tested and working. [...] Anthropic said that after feedback from the research preview, the company added scheduled scans for ongoing coverage, the ability to dismiss findings with documented reasons (essentially notes for future reviewers to triage analysis), and comma-separated and Markdown exports for easy import into existing audit systems.

title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5 With Faster Performance
summary: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5 With Faster Performance OpenAI has released GPT-5.5, a new version of its artificial intelligence model, positioning it as the most advanced system the company has developed so far. The update became available on April 23, 2026, and builds on improvements introduced in earli
content: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5 With Faster Performance [...] OpenAI has released GPT-5.5, a new version of its artificial intelligence model, positioning it as the most advanced system the company has developed so far. The update became available on April 23, 2026, and builds on improvements introduced in earlier versions. [...] According to the company, GPT-5.5 delivers faster responses, improved usability, and stronger performance across a wide range of tasks. These enhancements aim to make the system more accessible while increasing its effectiveness in complex applications. [...] GPT-5.5 is designed to handle advanced tasks that require deep knowledge and precision. These include scientific research, programming, mathematics, and data analysis. The system can process information more efficiently, allowing users to complete demanding tasks with greater speed and accuracy. [...] This upgrade replaces GPT-5.4, which was released in March 2026. The continuous updates reflect OpenAI’s strategy of refining its models through incremental improvements rather than waiting for entirely new systems. [...] The release of GPT-5.5 presents new opportunities for businesses, researchers, and developers. Faster processing and improved reasoning can enhance productivity, reduce costs, and accelerate innovation across industries. [...] GPT-5.5 highlights the rapid pace of innovation in artificial intelligence. As OpenAI continues to refine its models, the focus will likely shift toward building more integrated and versatile systems.

title: GPT-5.5 Launches as OpenAI’s Premium Agentic AI Model
summary: GPT-5.5 Launches as OpenAI’s Premium Agentic AI Model | Developments Today DTDevelopmentsToday Ask DT AIAudio BriefVideos[Podcast](https://developm
content: GPT-5.5 Launches as OpenAI’s Premium Agentic AI Model | Developments Today [...] AI & RoboticsOpenAI’s GPT-5.5 Arrives Framed as a More Agentic Model, With Pricing to Match [...] OpenAI launched GPT-5.5 on April 23 and positioned it as its most capable agentic AI model yet, emphasizing real-world work and agent use cases while charging roughly double the API price. [...] GPT-5.5 was launched on April 23 with a strong focus on agent use cases. [...] OpenAI’s release of GPT-5.5 on April 23 was presented not merely as another incremental model update, but as a stronger push into agentic AI. According to the framing cited by AI News, the company described GPT-5.5 as “a new class of intelligence for real work and powering agents,” and characterized it as its most capable agentic AI model yet. That wording is notable because it points to where competitive positioning in frontier AI has moved: less around general benchmark spectacle alone, and more around whether a model can reliably act, plan, and execute across real tasks. [...] The launch message also came with a harder commercial edge. GPT-5.5 was reported as carrying roughly double the API price. That means the release is not only a capability claim. It is also a pricing statement about how much the market should be willing to pay for more capable, more action-oriented systems. [...] Calling GPT-5. [...] most capable agentic [...] a strong claim [...] becomes part of [...] will absorb the [...] proves true will [...] -style behavior [...] Even without a full technical breakdown in the available source material, several things are clear from the positioning alone. First, OpenAI wants GPT-5.5 understood as a work model rather than just a chat model. Second, it sees agents as an important commercial layer rather than a side use case. Third, it is confident enough in the product’s differentiated value to support a materially higher API price. [...] For now, the release is most significant as a statement of direction. OpenAI is telling the market that the frontier is shifting toward agentic systems built for real work, and that the best of those systems may command a significantly higher price. GPT-5.5 is therefore not just another model name. It is a marker of how AI companies now want capability to be measured and sold. [...] OpenAI launched GPT-5.5 on April 23 and described it as a model for real work and agents. [...] AI News characterized it as OpenAI’s most capable agentic model yet. [...] The model was reported as costing about twice as much through the API.

title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, bringing company one step closer to an AI 'super app'
summary: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, bringing company one step closer to an AI 'super app' - iDigital News Buy Me a Coffee More No Result View All Result More No Result View All Result No Result View All Result OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, bringing company one step closer to an AI ‘super app’ Reading T
content: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, bringing company one step closer to an AI 'super app' - iDigital News [...] OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, bringing company one step closer to an AI ‘super app’ [...] OpenAI has officially unveiled its latest and most advanced artificial intelligence model, GPT-5.5, marking a significant stride towards the company’s ambitious vision of an AI ‘super app’. This new iteration is hailed as the ‘smartest and most intuitive to use model’ yet, promising enhanced capabilities across a multitude of applications for both businesses and consumers. The release underscores OpenAI’s relentless pursuit of innovation, bringing the prospect of a unified, multi-purpose AI service closer to reality. Greg Brockman, OpenAI President, discussing the future of AI. Image Credits: Jean Chung/Bloomberg / Getty Images [...] OpenAI’s pace of innovation has been nothing short of remarkable. The release of GPT-5.5 follows closely on the heels of its predecessor, GPT-5.4, which was introduced just last month. This rapid development cycle, with previous models also launched in quick succession in December and November, highlights the company’s commitment to continuous improvement. Jakub Pachocki, OpenAI’s chief scientist, noted the accelerated progress, stating, “We see pretty significant improvements in the short term, extremely significant improvements in the medium term… In fact, I would say, like, I think the last two years have been surprisingly slow.” This sentiment reflects the dynamic and competitive landscape of AI development, where each new model pushes the boundaries of what’s possible. [...] GPT-5. [...] Its increased capabilities [...] as agentic coding and [...] work, making it a powerful tool for complex business operations [...] Beyond enterprise applications, the model also shows significant promise [...] OpenAI’s internal data reveals that GPT- [...] consistently outperforms its [...] , as well as competing [...] Google’s Gemini 3.1 [...] Anthropic’ [...] Claude Opus 4. [...] presenting at an OpenAI event [...] GPT-5.5 is now widely available, according to OpenAI. The model is being rolled out to Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise users within ChatGPT, with GPT-5.5 Pro specifically targeting Pro, Business, and Enterprise subscribers. This broad accessibility ensures that a wide range of users can leverage the advanced capabilities of the new model. The company anticipates continued significant improvements in AI technology, promising an exciting future where AI plays an even more integral role in daily life and industry.

title: Anthropic Launches Claude Design, Integrates 3D Tools
summary: Anthropic Launches Claude Design, Integrates 3D Tools - MikeGingerich.com Anthropic has expanded its AI capabilities beyond code generation with a series of new creative tools designed to bridge the gap between concept and production. The company recently introduced Claude Design, a new Anthropic L
content: Anthropic Launches Claude Design, Integrates 3D Tools - MikeGingerich.com [...] Anthropic has expanded its AI capabilities beyond code generation with a series of new creative tools designed to bridge the gap between concept and production. The company recently introduced Claude Design, a new Anthropic Labs product that lets you collaborate with Claude to create polished visual work like designs, prototypes, slides, one-pagers, and more, while also announcing integrations with professional 3D modeling software including Autodesk Fusion, Blender, and SketchUp. [...] Anthropic launched Claude Design on April 17, 2026, rolling out the tool as a research preview to Claude Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise subscribers. The announcement had swift market consequences, with Figma’s stock falling about 7 percent on the launch day. The timing raised eyebrows across the design industry, as Anthropic CPO Mike Krieger had quietly resigned from Figma’s board three days earlier. [...] Claude Design is powered by Claude Opus 4.7, Anthropic’s most capable vision model, which the company describes as more tasteful and creative for professional tasks. The tool allows users to generate visual assets through natural language prompts, then refine outputs through conversation, inline comments, direct edits, or custom adjustment sliders. [...] Beyond Claude Design, Anthropic announced integrations with professional 3D modeling and creative software. The company is working with Autodesk Fusion, Blender, and SketchUp to enable AI-assisted workflows in 3D modeling environments, though specific details about these integrations remain limited in current announcements. [...] its new product is built for people who aren’t starting from a design tool and need to get from an idea to something visual quickly. The target users include founders, product managers, marketers, and anyone without formal design training who needs to create professional visual assets. [...] - Claude Design launched April 17, 2026 as a research preview for Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise subscribers - Figma stock dropped on launch day - Powered by Claude Opus 4.7 vision model - Automatically reads codebases and design files to extract and apply brand systems - Exports to Canva, PDF, PPTX, HTML, and hands off to Claude Code - Separate usage metering from standard Claude chat and code functions - Enterprise users receive one-time credit for approximately 20 prompts, valid until July 17, 2026 - Integrations announced with Autodesk Fusion, Blender, and SketchUp for 3D workflows [...] Anthropic launches Claude Design
